alerts.title: ₹48,762 spent at Croma in 3 transactions
alerts.description: Croma is your top merchant this month with ₹48,762.28 across just 3 purchases, making it the single largest merchant spend. Combined with ₹35,592.92 on Amazon (10 transactions), electronics and online shopping is driving your ₹1,35,546 Shopping category total.

alerts.title: Health & Fitness up 7.9% with pharmacy spend rising sharply
alerts.description: Health & Fitness hit ₹20,989.98 this month vs a 3-month average of ₹18,165.54 — a ₹2,824 overage. Apollo Pharmacy alone accounts for ₹10,974.38 across 10 visits, and MedPlus adds another ₹5,040.78 across 6 visits, totalling ₹16,015.16 in pharmacy spend this month.

alerts.title: Two overlapping pharmacy subscriptions: Apollo & MedPlus
alerts.description: You spent ₹10,974.38 at Apollo Pharmacy (10 transactions) and ₹5,040.78 at MedPlus (6 transactions) this month — a combined ₹16,015.16. Consolidating all pharmacy purchases to one provider could unlock loyalty rewards and reduce impulse top-ups.

alerts.title: LinkedIn Premium at ₹1,699/month — assess usage vs ChatGPT Plus
alerts.description: You are paying ₹1,699/month each for both LinkedIn Premium and ChatGPT Plus, totalling ₹3,398/month in AI/professional tool subscriptions. If LinkedIn Premium is not actively generating leads or job value, cancelling it saves ₹1,699 immediately.

tips: Your ATM withdrawals totalled ₹47,000 across 7 transactions this month — averaging ₹6,714 per withdrawal. Shifting these cash spends to UPI or card payments will make them trackable and help identify where this cash is actually going, since it currently falls into an unanalysed 'Others' bucket.

Food & Dining spend of ₹27,256 includes a Zomato subscription at ₹4,395/month plus ₹7,538.62 on Swiggy (15 orders) and ₹4,971.81 on Blinkit. Consider whether the Zomato Gold/Pro subscription is delivering enough free deliveries and discounts to justify ₹4,395 — if Swiggy is your primary ordering app, switching the subscription there or dropping it entirely could save ₹2,000–₹4,000/month.

Shopping at ₹1,35,546 is your largest category and still 14% above the 3-month average of ₹1,57,054 — but the April spike to ₹2,07,957 suggests binge-buying cycles. Set a monthly shopping cap of ₹1,20,000 and defer non-urgent Amazon and Flipkart (₹18,207) purchases to a weekly review day to avoid impulse buys across 16 combined transactions.

You spent ₹14,898.56 at IKEA in a single transaction this month. Large one-off home purchases like this are best planned with a sinking fund — setting aside ₹5,000/month into a dedicated home/furnishing budget would let you make these purchases without spiking your monthly totals unexpectedly.

Entertainment spending of ₹10,081 is 8.4% above the 3-month average of ₹9,304. BookMyShow (₹4,636 across 6 bookings) and INOX as a recurring ₹3,694/month subscription together account for the bulk of this. Evaluate whether a monthly INOX subscription makes sense alongside active BookMyShow usage — picking one primary cinema channel could save approximately ₹1,500–₹2,000/month.
